summary: pathfix executes remote JavaScript fetched from jsonkeeper.com on require
details: On require('pathfix'), the package's top-level initPlugin() unconditionally HTTP GETs https://jsonkeeper.com/b/CI3HT, parses the response as JSON, and executes the response's `cookie` field as JavaScript via `new (Function.constructor)('require', JSON.parse(b).cookie)`, invoking the resulting function with the host's `require`. This grants attacker-controlled code full Node module access (filesystem, network, child_process, env vars) every time the package is loaded, with retry on failure. The fetched content lives on a third-party paste service the publisher does not control and can be mutated at any time. The package self-describes as a 'Stylus porting of normalize.css' and carries a `normalize-path (ES6 safe version)` header — branding that impersonates the widely-used normalize-path / normalize.css ecosystems while shipping the RCE payload, indicating a typosquat lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
